guideline_name: CT Angiography of Chest, Heart, and Coronary Arteries

guidelines.criteria: CT Angiography of Chest, Heart, and Coronary Arteries
context: This policy applies to the Health Plan's lines of business.
Computed Tomographic Angiography (CTA) uses high-resolution CT technology with intravenous dye to visualize coronary arteries, cardiac structures, and non-cardiac thoracic vessels.
Definitions:

High Risk Non-Cardiac Surgery: Emergent procedures, particularly among the elderly, aortic and major lung surgeries, peripheral vascular surgeries, and procedures involving large fluid shifts or blood loss and expected to significantly affect hemodynamics.

Known Coronary Artery Disease (CAD): Includes history of myocardial infarction, revascularization, and the finding of either obstructive CAD or non-obstructive atherosclerotic plaque (<50% obstruction) on prior anatomic testing.

subcriteria.criteria: Specific Indications
Coronary CT Angiography (CPT Code 75574)
context: Includes bypass grafts when present.
Requires use of at least 64-slice scanners.

subcriteria.criteria: Assessment of obstructive CAD in intermediate-risk patients with acute chest pain, no known CAD, and normal findings or prior mild ischemia
context: Patient must be intermediate-risk.
Patient must have acute chest pain.
Patient must have NO known CAD.
Findings must be normal OR prior stress testing within past year indicated mild ischemia.
subcriteria.query: Is the request to assess the presence, extent, and severity of obstructive CAD in an intermediate-risk patient with acute chest pain who has no known CAD and either normal findings or prior stress testing (within the past year) indicative of mild ischemia?

subcriteria.criteria: Assessment of plaque progression in intermediate-risk patients with acute chest pain and known nonobstructive CAD
context: Patient must be intermediate-risk.
Patient must have acute chest pain.
Patient must have KNOWN nonobstructive CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is the request to assess progression of plaque and obstructive CAD in an intermediate-risk patient with acute chest pain who has known nonobstructive CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Initial test for CAD diagnosis/risk stratification in intermediate- to high-risk patients with stable chest pain and no known CAD
context: Patient must be intermediate- to high-risk.
Patient must have stable chest pain.
Patient must have NO known CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is this an initial test to diagnose CAD, stratify risk, and plan treatment in an intermediate- to high-risk patient with stable chest pain and no known CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Alternative to invasive coronary angiography in intermediate- to high-risk patients with stable chest pain and no known CAD after stress testing
context: Patient must be intermediate- to high-risk.
Patient must have stable chest pain.
Patient must have NO known CAD.
Stress testing was performed.

subcriteria.criteria: Inconclusive stress test
subcriteria.query: Was the prior stress test inconclusive?

subcriteria.criteria: Moderate to severe ischemia found, but symptoms persist despite optimization of preventive therapies
subcriteria.query: Did the stress test find moderate to severe ischemia, and do symptoms persist despite optimization of preventive therapies?

subcriteria.criteria: Assessment following abnormal or inconclusive stress testing in intermediate- to high-risk patients with stable chest pain and no known CAD
context: Patient must be intermediate- to high-risk.
Patient must have stable chest pain.
Patient must have NO known CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is the request to assess obstructive CAD following abnormal or inconclusive stress testing in an intermediate- to high-risk patient with stable chest pain and no known CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Assessment following negative stress testing in intermediate- to high-risk patients with stable chest pain, no known CAD, but high clinical suspicion
context: Patient must be intermediate- to high-risk.
Patient must have stable chest pain.
Patient must have NO known CAD.
High clinical suspicion of CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is the request to assess obstructive CAD following negative stress testing in an intermediate- to high-risk patient with stable chest pain, no known CAD, but high clinical suspicion of CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Assessment of plaque burden/progression in patients with stable chest pain and known nonobstructive CAD
context: Patient must have stable chest pain.
Patient must have KNOWN nonobstructive CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is the request to assess plaque burden and progression to obstructive CAD and guide treatment in a patient with stable chest pain and known nonobstructive CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Pre-operative CAD assessment for high-risk non-cardiac surgery
context: Patient must be stable, higher-risk.
Scheduled for high-risk non-cardiac surgery (emergent, elderly, aortic/major lung, peripheral vascular, large fluid shifts).
In lieu of stress test OR if invasive angiography is not feasible.
subcriteria.query: Is this for pre-operative CAD assessment of a stable, higher-risk patient scheduled to undergo high-risk non-cardiac surgery, in lieu of a stress test or because invasive coronary angiography is not feasible?

subcriteria.criteria: Calcium Score Testing (CPT 75571)
Exclude calcified plaque in low-risk patients
context: Low-risk patients.
Stable chest pain.
No known CAD.
subcriteria.query: Is the test intended to exclude calcified plaque in a low-risk patient with stable chest pain and no known CAD?

subcriteria.criteria: Intermediate- to high-risk patients with mild ischemia on stress testing
context: Intermediate- to high-risk patients.
Stable chest pain.
No known CAD.
Mild ischemia found upon stress testing.
subcriteria.query: Is the patient an intermediate- to high-risk individual with stable chest pain and no known CAD who was found to have mild ischemia upon stress testing?

subcriteria.criteria: Decision-making regarding statin therapy
subcriteria.query: Is the test intended to aid in decision-making for a patient uncertain or reluctant to initiate or intensify statin therapy?

subcriteria.criteria: Refine risk assessment in asymptomatic patients
context: Asymptomatic patients.
Borderline or intermediate risk.
subcriteria.query: Is the test intended to refine risk assessment, determine the need for subsequent testing, and guide management in an asymptomatic patient with borderline or intermediate risk?

subcriteria.criteria: Cardiac CT (Non-coronary; CPT Code 75572)
context: Performed with at least a 64-slice scanner.
To define details of cardiac morphology.

subcriteria.criteria: Pre-procedure pulmonary vein mapping for atrial fibrillation ablation
subcriteria.query: Is the scan for pre-procedure pulmonary vein mapping prior to catheter ablation for atrial fibrillation?

subcriteria.criteria: Post-procedure evaluation of pulmonary veins
subcriteria.query: Is the scan for post-procedure evaluation of pulmonary veins following isolation/ablation for atrial fibrillation to rule out pulmonary vein occlusion?

subcriteria.criteria: Pre-procedural determination for biventricular pacemaker insertion
subcriteria.query: Is the scan for pre-procedural determination of coronary vein morphology and cardiac anatomy prior to biventricular pacemaker insertion?

subcriteria.criteria: Pre-procedure evaluation for LAA closure device
context: In lieu of transesophageal echocardiography (TEE).
subcriteria.query: Is the scan for pre-procedure evaluation of the left atrial appendage (LAA), pulmonary vein, and other cardiac structures prior to placement of a LAA closure device (in lieu of TEE)?

subcriteria.criteria: Limitations and Exclusions
Ordering provider requirements
subcriteria.query: Was the study ordered by a provider other than a physician or qualified non-physician practitioner?

subcriteria.criteria: Supervision requirements
subcriteria.query: Will the image-acquisition portion of testing be performed without a Board Certified (ABMS) Cardiologist or Radiologist present?

subcriteria.criteria: Duplicative testing
subcriteria.query: Is the selection of CTA duplicative of other testing modalities or not needed to advance patient management decisions?

subcriteria.criteria: Routine general screening
subcriteria.query: Is the CT angiography being used for routine general screening of an asymptomatic patient (apart from covered indications)?

subcriteria.criteria: Separate billing for 3D rendering
context: 3D rendering is incorporated into coding descriptions.
subcriteria.query: Is there a request for separate billing for 3D rendering (CPT 76377)?
